text: A ferry crossing, run by an old man who still charges in pre-Broadcast coin. He doesn't ask questions. Neither do you.
Catfish in the shallows. The bayou is too thick for drones — their sensors choke on the humidity. Elias knows how to read the water.
Enforcer Units at the bridge toll point. They're not looking for you specifically. They're looking for anyone.
The Gulf storm rolls in fast. You spend the night bailing water out of the truck bed. Half the dried rations are ruined.
A bait shop off the highway, locked but not reinforced. Inside: fuel cans, boxed crackers, a first aid kit still in its wrapper.
An old juke box in a roadhouse, solar-powered. Someone's left it running. Deja finds a song she recognizes and doesn't say from where.
The heat and the wet take a toll. Someone's running a fever by morning.
